sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.23 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: An automorphism$$of a group$G$is called a nil-automorphism\/ if, for every$a G$, there exists$n$such that$[a,\, _n ] = 1$. Here$[x,\, _1 y]=[x,y]$and$[x,\, _{i+1} y]=[[x,\, _i y],y]$. An automorphism$$is called an$e$-auto\-mor\-phism\/ if, for any two$0.1ex$-inva\-ri\-ant subgroups$A$and$B$such that$A B$, there exists$a A B$such that$[a, ] B$. Is every$e$-auto\-mor\-phism of a group a nil-automorp...
